cualitativo.estrategia: The Sub-Fund is positioned to withstand a broad range of different environments. We remain conservatively positioned, with a relatively low weighting to equities and significant exposure to more defensive assets in the form of gold, short-dated inflation-linked bonds, and liquidity. We are also poised to act as and when opportunities arise, and will become more aggressive in increasing equity exposure if the current dislocation intensifies.
cualitativo.historia_fondo: The sub-fund was launched on 13 February 2012. The Strategy has greatly benefited from opportunistically buying from those indiscriminately selling in the past.
cualitativo.tipo_activos: The fund invests in equities (e.g., Agilent Technologies, Alphabet, Chubb, Diageo, Adobe, L’Oréal, Hubbell, Canadian National Railway, LSEG, LVMH), and defensive assets such as gold, short-dated inflation-linked bonds, liquidity, and short-maturity Japanese Government Bonds.
cualitativo.filosofia_inversion: Our approach is always to remain patient and lean into risk when we are rewarded to do so. The Strategy has greatly benefited from opportunistically buying from those indiscriminately selling in the past and should continue to do so in future, as we look to fulfil our dual mandate of preserving our investors’ capital and growing it in real terms over the long term.
cualitativo.objetivos_reales: The sub-fund aims to achieve growth in capital, ahead of inflation (UK Retail Prices Index), over the longer term (5 to 7 years). We look to fulfil our dual mandate of preserving our investors’ capital and growing it in real terms over the long term.
cualitativo.proceso_seleccion: Within the equity component of the portfolio, we added to existing holdings and established new holdings. The majority of these purchases were made when equity markets had fallen in April. We have initiated several new investments and will become more aggressive in increasing equity exposure if the current dislocation intensifies. The Strategy has greatly benefited from opportunistically buying from those indiscriminately selling in the past.

periodos.contexto_mercado: The period saw the return of Donald Trump as US President, leading to unprecedented tariffs in April and a sharp sell-off in equity markets. This hardline stance on trade shocked markets, causing US dollar, Treasury bonds, and US equities to fall. Erratic news flow exacerbated uncertainty, and the tariff burden may trigger an economic slowdown. Aggressive trade actions damaged the historic safe-haven status of US assets. Geopolitical tensions in the Middle East between Israel and Iran caused oil prices to rise from $60 in early May to almost $80 in June, before retreating after US intervention.
periodos.tesis_gestora: The Sub-Fund is positioned to withstand a broad range of different environments, with an approach to remain patient and lean into risk when rewarded. The manager believes the damage to the US's credibility as a safe-haven could sustain. The outlook suggests President Trump’s America First agenda is reshaping geopolitics and global trade, leading to uncertainty and a growing risk of an economic slowdown. Equities are not cheap, and corporate earnings and valuations may rebase lower if growth falters. The strategy remains conservatively positioned but is poised to act opportunistically if dislocation intensifies, aiming to preserve capital and grow it in real terms over the long term.
periodos.decisiones_tomadas:
Equity component:
- Added to existing holdings: Agilent Technologies, Alphabet, Chubb, Diageo.
- Established new holdings: Adobe, L’Oréal, Hubbell, Canadian National Railway, LSEG, LVMH. (Majority of these purchases were made when equity markets had fallen in April).
- Exited positions: Procter & Gamble (P&G) and Moody’s, due to uncertainties about future growth in earnings and risk to current valuations (both were profitable investments).
Gold:
- Modestly reduced the holding.
Bonds/Currencies:
- Reduced the Sub-Fund’s weighted average duration to US Treasuries from five years to two years.
- Trimmed the net US dollar exposure from 25% to 9%.
- Added two Japanese Government Bonds with a maturity of less than two years, based on the yen being cheap relative to sterling and its potential benefit in a risk-off environment.
periodos.resultado_real: The Trojan Fund (Ireland) Class O GBP accumulation shares returned +0.5% in the financial period. During the sharp equity market sell-off in April, the Sub-Fund fell considerably less than the Index, declining -2.7% (peak-to-trough) versus -12.8% for the FTSE All-Share Index (TR), attributed to its defensive asset allocation.

periodos.contexto_mercado: The year 2025 saw strong stock markets overall (FTSE All-Share +24%, S&P 500 +18%), aided by a weaker dollar and opportunities outside the US. However, there were strongly divergent performances by company, with 'quality' equities underperforming significantly. A technical bear market occurred with the S&P 500 falling -20% from February to April. US tariffs were the single greatest driver of market volatility. Gold experienced sustained demand despite higher prices. The dollar depreciated over -9% against a basket of trading partners. The importance of AI grew, leading to increased market concentration, with the top 10% of US stocks accounting for 78% of total market capitalisation.
periodos.tesis_gestora: The trend of de-dollarisation, which began over a quarter of a century ago, has accelerated and is expected to continue as the world shifts its centre of gravity away from the United States. Central bank demand for gold has been strengthened. The yen is expected to behave inversely to equity markets due to a large carry trade and its multi-year low valuation, but Japanese authorities will tolerate limited further weakness. Regarding AI, much caution is warranted due to elevated valuations and little known at this stage; its success depends on enterprise deployment, and bottlenecks in infrastructure build (labour, power, land) are limiting factors. There is little revenue or profit to show for the significant investment in AI so far.
periodos.decisiones_tomadas: Increased equity allocation by around 10 percentage points, taking advantage of lower valuations (half done on April 7th). Maintained gold holding at circa 12% throughout the year. Reduced net dollar exposure from 25% to 8% in the weeks following April 2nd. Reduced US TIPS in favour of UK Linkers and reduced duration materially. Built a 10% holding in short-dated Japanese government bonds by the end of the year. Added to UK Linkers.
periodos.resultado_real: The strategy delivered returns of around 9-10% for the calendar year. Equities performed respectably, rising +10% in local currency terms. During the Feb-Apr 2025 S&P 500 bear market (-20%), the strategy fell just over -2%. The increase in equity allocation on April 7th coincided with a low point for equity indices. Gold returned +65% in dollars or +53% in sterling. The dollar depreciated over -9%.
periodos.notas: The report refers to 'The strategy' and 'Our equities' which are representative of Troy Asset Management Limited’s Multi-asset Strategy, under which the Trojan Fund (Ireland) is managed.

periodos.contexto_mercado: Inflation continues to be above target in both the US and the UK, even as central banks ease monetary policy. There is a material risk that economic growth is robust but the cost of capital, as determined by the bond market, rises. Concerns exist regarding the US Treasury market. AI valuations remain elevated, with the most extreme excesses found in private markets.
periodos.tesis_gestora: Inflation will be structurally embedded and facilitated by policymakers due to central banks relinquishing their inflation targets and the undermining of US central bank independence. An easy monetary policy trajectory risks becoming unsustainable if bond markets start to worry about inflation, and any hint of higher rates would be negatively received. A material risk exists that robust economic growth could lead to a rise in the cost of capital, with significant implications for equity markets. Private markets represent the greatest point of fragility due to areas of excess, with AI at the intersection of many current fragilities.
periodos.decisiones_tomadas: Remain defensive with just over 40% in equities. Retain just under a third of the portfolio in index-linked bonds (both UK and US). Maintain deliberately short duration at just under two years, reflecting fiscal and geopolitical risks. Maintain approximately 20% in liquidity to take advantage of future volatility. Equity additions in the past year focused on stocks outside the current AI narrative or those with resilient business models, guided by valuation.
periodos.notas: This period reflects the manager's current positioning and forward-looking views as of the report date.

periodos.contexto_mercado: In September, the yield on the 10-year US Treasury bond reached 4.8%, its highest level since 2007, precipitating weakness across asset classes. Expectations shifted to rates staying above 5% until September 2024. There's an emerging acceptance that the 'Goldilocks world' has given way to a more bearish reality, with the Federal Reserve committed to keeping rates high. Equity valuations fell, impacting 'bond proxies'. The consensus earnings growth for most listed companies doesn't account for a recession, despite historical patterns showing recessions often follow rate hiking cycles with a lag. Businesses, particularly small ones, face financial distress due to rising borrowing costs (4% to 10%) and tightening lending standards by regional US banks.
periodos.tesis_gestora: We are sceptical that 'immaculate disinflation' can be achieved without higher rates impacting growth. We believe rates will remain structurally higher and inflation higher and less stable in the future. Our view on higher rates means the price paid for investments is crucial. We anticipate opportunities to buy at lower valuations will coincide with earnings deterioration. We maintain conviction in the longer-run durability of exceptional business models, making us optimistic about building equity exposure. We also see persistent opportunities in digitisation (cloud computing, digital payments, AI) and the growing wealth of Asian consumers.
periodos.decisiones_tomadas: As real yields rose, we added to our index-linked holdings. Specifically, in August, we initiated holdings in US and UK 2026-maturing bonds at positive real yields of over 2.5% and over 1.5% respectively. (Prior to this, we had not owned UK index-linked bonds since 2019). We are optimistic about building on the current 25% portfolio allocation to equities.
periodos.resultado_real: The portfolio generated modestly positive returns year-to-date. The index-linked bond asset class overall was around flat in terms of contribution to returns, which was 'a little disappointing' but expected given rising interest rate expectations. However, most shorter-dated index-linked bonds in the portfolio generated a positive absolute return, largely due to inflation accrual (principal value rising as inflation remained strong), which offset the headwind of rising real yields.

actuales.nombre: LVMH Moet Hennesy Louis Vuitton SE
